{"type":"task_assignment","taskId":"4","subject":"Implement Spanish translations in es.json","description":"Implement all missing and untranslated Spanish translations in i18n/es.json.

You will receive a report from the es-finder agent with browser-verified findings. But start immediately with the known missing keys below - these are confirmed missing from the JSON comparison.

WORKING DIRECTORY: /Users/ggomes/dev/pnmui-git/.claude/worktrees/i18n

FILE TO EDIT: i18n/es.json

REFERENCE FILE: i18n/en.json (English source of truth)

TRANSLATION GUIDELINES:
- Use Latin American Spanish consistently
- Match the tone/formality of existing translations in es.json
- Technical terms like MSISDN, URL, TCAP, CDR, HTTP, JSON, XML, Unicode, PCC should stay in English
- "Host" can stay as "Host" in Spanish (common usage)
- UI terms: Save = Guardar, Cancel = Cancelar, Delete = Eliminar, Add = Agregar, Edit = Editar
- Keep interpolation variables like {{count}} intact
- Preserve the nested JSON structure matching en.json exactly

MISSING KEYS TO ADD (74 keys that exist in en.json but not in es.json):
All spcm.* keys listed below need Spanish translations added to es.json:
- spcm.planDefinition.section.planIdentity = "Identidad del Plan"
- spcm.planDefinition.section.usageAllowance = "Asignacion de Uso"
- spcm.planDefinition.section.pricing = "Precios"
- spcm.planDefinition.section.planType = "Tipo de Plan"
- spcm.planDefinition.section.renewals = "Renovaciones"
- spcm.planDefinition.section.rollover = "Acumulacion"
- spcm.planDefinition.section.expiry = "Vencimiento"
- spcm.planDefinition.step.0-4 (wizard step names)
- spcm.planDefinition.saveAndFinish, savePlanFirst, requiredNote, etc.
- spcm.usageCounterDefinition.*, spcm.pccProfile.*, spcm.enum.*, spcm.filter.*, spcm.status.*, spcm.pagination.*, spcm.versionsDrawer.*, spcm.cloneDialog.*, spcm.button.*

ALSO FIX untranslated values (31 keys where ES value equals EN value):
- common.accordion.general = "General" -> stays as "General" (same in Spanish)
- smsc.ss7Route.pointCode = "Point Code" -> "Codigo de Punto"
- smsc.applicationProfiles.blackouts = "Blackouts" -> "Bloqueos"
- spcm.planDefinition.section.general = "General" -> stays as "General"
- spcm.enum.state.EDITABLE = "Editable" -> stays as "Editable" (same in Spanish)
- spcm.enum.usageScope.PLAN = "Plan" -> stays as "Plan" (same in Spanish)

Read en.json and es.json first to understand the structure, then edit es.json to add all missing translations.

When you receive additional findings from es-finder, incorporate those too. When done, send a completion message to the team lead.","assignedBy":"team-lead","timestamp":"2026-03-06T05:29:33.657Z"}

{"type":"task_assignment","taskId":"8","subject":"es-translator","description":"You are the Spanish Translator agent. Your job is to add all missing Spanish translations to i18n/es","assignedBy":"es-translator","timestamp":"2026-03-06T05:31:28.469Z"}

Here are all the untranslated English strings found in the SPCM app that need Spanish translations in es.json.

## 1. HARDCODED ENGLISH STRINGS (no t() call at all)

### Sidebar.tsx (apps/spcm/src/components/layout/Sidebar.tsx, lines 68-71)
These need `t()` wrapping AND es.json entries:
- "Main Menu" (line 68) - already in es.json as spcm.menu.main = "Menu Principal"
- "SPCM Dashboard" (line 69)
- "Plan Definitions" (line 71) - already in es.json as spcm.menu.planDefinitions

### PlanDefinitionForm.tsx action bar (lines 1895, 1903)
- `'Saving...'` is hardcoded when `saving` is true, bypasses t() - needs key common.button.saving

### PlanDefinitionForm.tsx classification dropdown (line 884-886)
- "Data", "Voice", "SMS" are hardcoded dropdown values (though these may be intentional as enum values)

## 2. MISSING TRANSLATION KEYS IN es.json

The following keys are used in source code via t() calls but DO NOT exist in es.json. They need to be added:

## 3. KEYS EXISTING IN es.json BUT USING WRONG VALUES
The es.json pccProfile translations use "Perfil de Servicio" etc. but the source code uses short form labels like "Service Flows", "QoS", "Charging Control", "Redirect Profile", "Network Parameters", "Device Info". The existing es.json translations for these should be reviewed to match the actual fallback strings.
summary: Complete list of untranslated SPCM strings for Spanish

text:
IMPORTANT: The pt-translator just added 86 NEW keys to en.json that were found in source code t() calls but missing from all translation files. These keys now exist in en.json and pt.json but NOT in es.json yet. You need to add Spanish translations for all of them too.

After you finish the original 74 missing keys, please re-read en.json and compare against es.json to find ALL keys that exist in en.json but not es.json, and add Spanish translations for every one. The new keys include:
- common.button.backToList, common.button.next
- spcm.header.dashboard, spcm.header.planDefinition
- spcm.dashboard.* (2 keys)
- spcm.planDefinition.*Help (27 help text keys)
- spcm.planDefinition.readOnlyBanner, creatingVersion, createNewVersion
- spcm.planDefinitions.* (24 list page keys)
- spcm.filter.planLabel
- spcm.pagination.first, pageInfo, last
- spcm.precedence.* (5 keys: lowest/low/medium/high/highest)
- spcm.pccProfile.viewTitle, refreshFailed
- spcm.usageCounterDefinition.* (7 keys)
- spcm.usageRuleDefinition.dialogTitle, refreshFailed
- spcm.validation.* (6 keys)

Please do a full diff of en.json vs es.json after your edits to ensure 0 missing keys remain.
summary: 86 new keys added to en.json, need ES translations

text:
BROWSER VISUAL CONFIRMATION of untranslated strings in Spanish mode.

I browsed the app at http://localhost:5177 with language set to Espanol. Here are the English strings I visually confirmed on screen:

## SIDEBAR (hardcoded - no t() calls, present on ALL pages):
- "Main Menu"
- "SPCM Dashboard"
- "Plan Definitions"

## DASHBOARD PAGE:
- "SPCM Dashboard" (title)
- "Service and Plan Configuration Management dashboard." (subtitle)

## PLAN DEFINITIONS LIST PAGE:
- "PLAN" (filter label, showing as uppercase - key: spcm.filter.planLabel)
- "Select a plan..." (dropdown)
- "View Versions" (button)
- "Live" / "Draft" (filter pills)
- "Plan Definitions" (page title header)
- "- Delete" (button)
- "+ Add New Plan Definition" (button)
- Table headers: "Name", "Status", "Version", "Last Updated", "Actions"
- Status badges: "Available", "Unavailable"
- Pagination: "First", "Previous", "Page 1 of 4 (Total Records: 68)", "Next", "Last"

## PLAN DEFINITION FORM - STEP 1 (Basic):
- Step labels: "Basic Plan Settings", "Advanced Plan Settings", "Policy Profiles", "Usage Counters", "Usage Rules"
- "Required fields are marked with an asterisk"
- Section headers: "Plan Identity", "Usage Allowance", "Pricing", "Plan Type", "Renewals"
- Helper texts: "Display name for this plan", "Unique identifier for this plan", "Category classification for this plan", "Detailed description of the plan features and benefits", "Brief summary of key plan features", "Type of usage measurement for this plan", "Maximum usage allowance for this plan", "Numeric unit amount or UNLIMITED"
- Labels: "Allowable usage", "Chargeable", "Core Plan", "Non Core Plan", "Unlimited", "Limited"
- "Next" button

## PLAN DEFINITION FORM - STEP 2 (Advanced):
- Same step labels as above
- "Required fields are marked with an asterisk"
- Helper texts: "When the plan should be activated", "Allow plan activation to be deferred to a later time"
- Labels: "Purchase Limits", "Rollover on Renewal", "Expiry Warning"
- Section headers: "Rollover", "Expiry"

## PLAN DEFINITION FORM - STEP 3 (Policy Profiles):
- Same step labels
- Info paragraph: "Configure Policy and Charging Control settings..."
- Group headers: "Settings", "Constraints"
- "Next" button

## PCC PROFILE DIALOG (Add):
- "Add PCC Profile" (dialog title)
- Section headers: "General Profile Settings", "Settings", "Constraints"
- Helper texts: "Threshold Profiles apply when invoked by Usage Rule Actions...", "Configure settings applied as part of this PCC Profile", "Configure constraints that must be satisfied...", "Percentage of data usage counted by Usage Counters..."
- Labels: "Service" (should be "Service Flows" / "Flujos de Servicio"), "Redirect Profile"

These are ALL visually confirmed in the browser. The es-translator should make sure all these keys have Spanish translations in es.json.
summary: Browser-confirmed untranslated Spanish strings with screenshots
